observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the
management justify the noted lapses.
The Municipal Accountant and
Treasurer shall exert all effort
necessary to prepare and submit the
financial statements the soonest
possible time considering that these
were already long-overdue.
Henceforth, submit all the financial
reports on or before the 10th day of the
ensuing month. Likewise, the
Municipal Engineer or the Municipal
Planning and Development
Coordinator see to it that the
required accomplishment reports as
provided for under the DILG and
COA Circulars are religiously
adhered to.

We have recommended that the
Municipality file an appeal for the Notices
of Disallowance amounting to
₱35,935,134.27 to the previous audit team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality filed an appeal for the Notices of Disallowance amounting to ₱35,935,134.27 to the previous audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the
disbursement vouchers and
liquidations of the Municipal
Treasurer be submitted immediately
to the Auditor for verification,
otherwise, management must cause
the suspension of payment of the
salaries of officials concerned until
they shall have complied with the
requirements of the Commission,
without prejudice to any disciplinary
action that may be instituted against
such official or employee pursuant to
Section 122 of PD 1445 and Art. 218
of the Revised Penal Code.

We have recommended that in order
to establish accurately and reliably
the balances of PPE accounts and
forestall any losses, determine the
actual existence and condition of
properties. In this regard, create an
Inventory Committee to oversee the
actual inventory taking procedures
and reconcile the report derived
therein with the balance shown in the
books of accounts. Moreover, submit
to the audit team the result of the
inventory taking through the Report
on the Physical Count of PPE
(RPCPPE) for verification.

We have recommended that in order
to establish accurately and reliably
the balances of local road networks
and the related existence and
condition thereof, management
should undertake the following:
 The Municipal Engineer and the
Property Custodian conduct a
yearly physical count of local road
network projects and consequently
update the local road map of the
LGU;
 The Municipal Engineer provide
the Municipal Accountant and the
Property Custodian the description
and cost segregation of road
components of various road
infrastructure projects; and
 The Municipal Accountant keeps
and maintain subsidiary ledger for
roads and its components and
reconcile the same to the result of
the physical inventory taking at the
end of each year.

We have recommended that efforts
should be exerted in the validation of
the mentioned accounts. Results of
the validation should be submitted to
the COA Audit Team for verification
and proper audit action.
We also have recommended that the
Municipal Accountant submit a
request for authority to write-off the
receivables together with the
necessary supporting document to
the Regional Director, Commission
on Audit for evaluation and possible
dropping from the books of
accounts.
For the dormant loans, we have
recommended that the Municipal
Accountant should try to trace the
nature of these liabilities and locate
source documents. If said payables
are not supported with documents to
establish the claims, adjusting entries
should be prepared to present fairly
the financial statements.

We have recommended that the
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
submit to the Commission on Audit
all financial reports and accounts
within the time prescribed by
regulations.

We have recommended that
management justify the noted lapses.
The Municipal Mayor instruct the
MPDC to discuss with the LDC the
preparation and adoption of both
plans for CY 2017 onwards.

We have recommended that the
management justify the noted
noted lapses and henceforth, adhere
to the transparency provisions as
provided by the different rules and
regulations cited above.

We have recommended that
management submit the missing CY
2016 contracts/POs and henceforth,
ensure that copies of duly perfected
purchase orders/contracts are
submitted within five (5) working
days from issuance thereof to the
Auditor for prompt review and
evaluation as required under COA
Circular No. 2009-001.

We have recommended that
management justify the purchase of
reconditioned instead of brand new
dump truck as approved by the
respective local sangguniang bayan
as well as non-conduct of technical
study before the purchase.
Moreover, we have recommended
that management submit the contract
and supporting documents of the
procured dump truck for proper
review and evaluation. Henceforth,
adhere to the provisions of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the
conduct of public bidding as well as
the adoption of alternative mode of
procurement.

We have recommended that to
establish accurately the balances of
PPE accounts and forestall any
losses, determine the actual existence
and condition of properties, the
Municipal Mayor form a committee
which will oversee the actual
inventory taking procedures and
reconcile the report derived therein
with the balance shown in the books
of accounts.

We have recommended that the
disbursement vouchers and
liquidations of the Municipal
Treasurer be submitted immediately
to the Auditor for verification,
otherwise, management must cause
the suspension of payment of the
salaries of officials concerned until
they shall have complied with the
requirements of the Commission,
without prejudice to any disciplinary
action that may be instituted against
such official or employee pursuant to
Section 122 of PD 1445 and Art. 218
of the Revised Penal Code.
Henceforth, disbursement vouchers/
liquidation report must be submitted
within 10 days after the end of each
month together with the journals and
reports of disbursement.

In view of the noted violations we
have recommended that management
justify the noted lapses, submit the
required documentary requirements
and henceforth all fund transfers to
LGUs should be supported by
Memorandum of Agreement.
Moreover, the recipient barangays
should submit the liquidation report
for the fund transfers in 2014 and
2015.

We have recommended that the
agency submit a copy of Annual
Procurement Plan (APP) of the
agency for 2015 and henceforth, an
APP should be prepared and
approved before any procurement is
made.

We have have recommended the
following:
> Justify the noted lapses;
> Utilize the LDRRMF for
programs, projects and activities
which conform to the objective of
the fund;
> Submit the necessary supporting
documents to validate the
distribution of goods to intended
beneficiaries; and
> Require the Sangguniang Bayan
to pass a resolution for the
opening of a special trust fund
account for the unexpended
balance of the fund.

1.7 We recommended that management:
1.7.1 Submit the Financial Statements and Disbursement Vouchers for
January to December 2018 and all other reports/documents
enumerated in item 1.3 on or before March 18, 2019;
1.7.2 Determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to
comply with the required submission dates of accounts and reports;
and
1.7.3 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer exert all necessary efforts to render the agency’s
accounts within the prescribed period.

2.8 We recommended that management justify the noted lapses and
henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the
above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the
Municipal Accountant and
Treasurer:
 Make representation with the
concerned government agencies
with regard to the discrepancies
noted in their records as against the
books of the LGU and make
adjustments accordingly;
 Remit immediately the determined
unpaid obligations to the concerned
government agencies; and
 Henceforth, conduct periodic
reconciliation of balances in order
to avoid such discrepancies.

We have recommended that the
Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer submit duly
accomplished supporting documents
as required by regulations.
Succeeding transactions should be
properly supported with complete
documentation in order to establish
the validity of claims. Processing
and payment of claims that are not
properly documented should be
discontinued.

We have recommended that
Management direct the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that upon
Disbursement Vouchers for
government contracts are duly and
properly supported before payments
are made pursuant to Section 4.6 of
PD No. 1445.

We have recommended that
management strictly comply with the
said regulation by replacing the
Municipal Accountant-Designate
with another officer as regular
member of the BAC, who is at least
a fifth ranking permanent official, as
required under Sec. 11.2.1, IRR-A of
RA 9184.

We have recommended that the Bids
and Awards Committee justify the
noted lapses, submit the needed
documents for verification and
henceforth, comply with the rules
and regulations as required by R.A.
9184.

We have recommended that:
1. The 20% development fund
should be appropriated and
utilized for programs and projects
that are considered capital or
investment in nature;
2. Henceforth, management should
strictly observe the pertinent
provisions of DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1
dated April 13, 2011 on the
appropriation and utilization of the
20% of the annual IRA for
development.

We have recommended that
Management:
 Justify the noted lapses and
henceforth familiarize themselves
with the relevant provisions with
regard to the utilization of the
LDRRM Funds;
 See to it that No action For
unexpended/unobligated funds
from prior years LDRRM are
treated as Trust Liability at the end
of the year and duly transferred to a
special trust fund account; and
 Determine the correct unexpended
balance of the LDRRMF and
prepare the necessary adjustments.

We have recommended that
management justify the noted lapses.
The Municipal Engineer designate
was also advised to continuously
update himself with the new bidding
guidelines for infrastructure projects
and henceforth abide to the
provisions of DPWH Dept. Order
No. 11 S. 2014

We have recommended the
following:
 The Municipal Mayor creates a
GFPS Execom, TWG and
Secretariat that will supervise,
advocate and install a GAD Focal
Point System (GFPS) as well as the
required Gender Responsive
Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E)
System;
 The Municipal Mayor with the
help of the Sangguniang Bayan,
formulate and adapt a Local GAD
Code;
 The MSWDO being the leading
authority in identifying gender
related issues and concerns, see to
it that GAD activities are identified
through the use of HGDG and that
the GAD plan are regularly
updated; and
 Henceforth, management sees to it
that said plan and budget are
approved by the DILG and the
PPDO.

We have recommended that the
Municipal Accountant record
transactions in accordance with the
prescribed by COA Circular 2015-009
or the Revised Chart of Accounts for
Local Government Units. Fund
transfers should be debited to Due
from LGUs and will only be credited
upon submission of liquidation
documents by recipient agencies.

We have recommended the
following:
- The Municipal Treasurer should
be granted only cash advances for
payroll, honoraria and petty
operating expenses;
- Payments out of cash advance
should not exceed ₱15,000.00 and
purchase of goods, repair and
maintenance of vehicles and
payment of terminal leave benefits
should be paid directly thru checks;
- A petty cash fund should be
maintained for payment of expenses
not exceeding ₱1,000.00 and claims
above ₱1,000 should be paid through
check as provided under Paragraph 3,
Item IX, Section 83 of the Local
Treasury Manual;
- Transfer of cash advances from
one accountable officer to another
should be stopped.
- Cash advances should be granted
only to designated Disbursing
Officers, and that elective officials
be granted cash advances only for
travelling expenses. Henceforth,
granting of cash advances to the
Municipal Mayor should be stopped.
- The Municipal Accountant trace
back transactions in prior years to
determine the nature and the
persons accountable for the cash
advances amounting to ₱317,410.72 and demand the liquidation or return thereof.

We have recommended that the
Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution
authorizing the Municipality of
Malibcong to open a separate bank
account for the Share from Tobacco
Excise Tax. Further the municipality
should maintain a separate book for
the said fund as well as establish a
complete set of ledgers to account
for its transactions.

We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of PPE accounts and forestall any losses, determine the actual existence and condition of properties.  In this regard, create an Inventory Committee to oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of accounts. Moreover, submit to the audit team the result of the inventory taking through the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for verification.

We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of local road networks and the related existence and condition thereof, management should undertake the following:

The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and 
The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of each year.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit to the Commission on Audit all financial reports and accounts within the time prescribed by regulations.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.
We have recommended that the management justify the noted the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

We have recommended that management submit the missing CY 2016 contracts/POs and henceforth, ensure that copies of duly perfected purchase orders/contracts are submitted within five (5) working days from issuance thereof to the Auditor for prompt review and evaluation as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses, submit the needed documents for verification and henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that management justify the purchase of reconditioned instead of brand new dump truck as approved by the respective local sangguniang bayan as well as non-conduct of technical study before the purchase.

Moreover, we have recommended that management submit the contract and supporting documents of the procured dump truck for proper review and evaluation.   Henceforth, adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the conduct of public bidding as well as the adoption of alternative mode of procurement.

4.11	We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer shall exert all effort necessary to prepare and submit the financial statements the soonest possible time considering that these were already long-overdue. Henceforth, submit all the financial reports on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer or the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator see to it that the required accomplishment reports as provided for under the DILG and COA Circulars are religiously adhered to.

We have recommended that the disbursement vouchers and liquidations of the Municipal Treasurer be submitted immediately to the Auditor for verification, otherwise, management must cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445 and Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We have recommended that efforts should be exerted in the validation of the mentioned accounts.  Results of the validation should be submitted to the COA Audit Team for verification and proper audit action.

We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit a request for authority to write-off the receivables together with the necessary supporting document to the Regional Director, Commission on Audit for evaluation and possible dropping from the books of accounts. For the dormant loans, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should try to trace the nature of these liabilities and locate source documents. If said payables are not supported with documents to establish the claims, adjusting entries should be prepared to present fairly the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that to establish accurately the balances of PPE accounts and forestall any losses, determine the actual existence and condition of properties, the Municipal Mayor form a committee which will oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of accounts.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit a request for authority to write-off the receivables together with the necessary supporting document to the Regional Director, Commission on Audit for evaluation and possible dropping from the books of accounts. For the dormant loans, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should try to trace the nature of these liabilities and locate source documents. If said payables are not supported with documents to establish the claims, adjusting entries should be prepared to present fairly the financial statements.

We have have recommended that the Municipal Accountant record transactions in accordance with the prescribed by COA Circular 2015-009 or the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units. Fund transfers should be debited to Due from LGUs and will only be credited upon submission of liquidation documents by recipient agencies.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant:            
>    Make representation with the concerned government agencies with regards to the discrepancies noted in their records as against the books of the LGU and adjust accordingly.
>   Remit immediately the determined unpaid obligations to the concerned government agencies
>   Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of balances

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by regulations. Succeeding transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation   in order to establish the validity of claims. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.

We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for government contracts are duly and properly supported with the required documents as prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit to the Commission on Audit all financial reports and accounts within the time prescribed by regulations.

In view of the noted violations we have recommended that management justify the noted lapses, submit the required documentary requirements and henceforth all fund transfers to LGUs should be supported by Memorandum of Agreement. Moreover, the recipient barangays should submit the liquidation report for the fund transfers in 2014 and 2015.

We have recommended that the management submit copies of duly perfected purchase orders/contracts within five (5) working days from issuance thereof to the Auditor for prompt review and evaluation as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.
We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency for 2015 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses, submit the needed documents for verification and henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipality of Malibcong to open a separate bank account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Further the municipality should maintain a separate book for the said fund as well as establish a complete set of ledgers to account for its transactions.

We have recommended the following:                                    
-  Justify the noted lapses and submit the required supporting documents
-    Specifically identify and prepare a detailed list of the priority projects to be funded under the 20% development fund which should include particular location and time frame of implementation. Guidelines and policies involving the implementation of these programs or project should have been included in the planning and budgeting.
-    Submit the Program of Works as supporting documents to the Annual Investment Plan to aid in the further analysis of Appropriated Projects vis-a-vis actual expenditures. For lump sum appropriation, authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan should be obtained.
-     Implement only projects set forth in the Annual Investment Program. In instances where other priority projects are identified for implementation, a Sangguniang Bayan resolution should be passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual Investment Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan. 
-     Strictly comply with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the preparation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain the desired socio-economic and environmental condition of constituents.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Engineer is also advised to continuously update himself with the new guidelines for infrastructure projects and henceforth abide to the provisions of DPWH Dept. Order No. 11 S. 2014

1.9	We have recommended that the disbursement vouchers and liquidations of the Municipal Treasurer be submitted immediately to the Auditor for verification, otherwise, management must cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445 and Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
1.10	Henceforth, disbursement vouchers/liquidation report must be submitted within 10 days after the end of each month together with the journals and reports of disbursement.

2.7	We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of PPE accounts and forestall any losses, determine the actual existence and condition of properties.  In this regard, create an Inventory Committee to oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of accounts. Moreover, submit to the audit team the result of the inventory taking through the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for verification.

3.10	We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of local road networks and the related existence and condition thereof, management should undertake the following:
The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of each year.

4.7	We have recommended that efforts should be exerted in the validation of the mentioned accounts.  Results of the validation should be submitted to the COA Audit Team for verification and proper audit action.
4.8	We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit a request for authority to write-off the receivables together with the necessary supporting document to the Regional Director, Commission on Audit for evaluation and possible dropping from the books of accounts. For the dormant loans, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should try to trace the nature of these liabilities and locate source documents. If said payables are not supported with documents to establish the claims, adjusting entries should be prepared to present fairly the financial statements.

5.7	In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer:
Make representation with the concerned government agencies with regard to the discrepancies noted in their records as against the books of the LGU and make adjustments accordingly;
Remit immediately the determined unpaid obligations to the concerned government agencies; and
Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of balances in order to avoid such discrepancies.

6.9	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by regulations. Succeeding transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.

7.6	We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for government contracts are duly and properly supported before payments are made pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445.
8.10	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit to the Commission on Audit all financial reports and accounts within the time prescribed by regulations.
9.9	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.
10.5	We have recommended that the management justify the noted the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

11.6	We have recommended that management submit the missing CY 2016 contracts/POs and henceforth, ensure that copies of duly perfected purchase orders/contracts are submitted within five (5) working days from issuance thereof to the Auditor for prompt review and evaluation as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

12.5	We have recommended that management strictly comply with the said regulation by replacing the Municipal Accountant-Designate with another officer as regular member of the BAC, who is at least a fifth ranking permanent official, as required under Sec. 11.2.1,  IRR-A of RA 9184.

13.13	We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses, submit the needed documents for verification and henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184.

14.8	In view of the above mentioned audit observations, we have recommended that management justify the purchase of reconditioned instead of brand new dump truck as approved by the respective local sanggunian as well as non-conduct of technical study before the purchase.
14.9	Moreover, we have recommended that management submit the contract and supporting documents of the procured dump truck for proper review and evaluation.   Henceforth, adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the conduct of public bidding as well as the adoption of alternative mode of procurement.

16.5	In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that Management:
Justify the noted lapses and henceforth, familiarize themselves with the relevant provisions with regard to the utilization of the LDRRM Funds;
See to it that unexpended/unobligated funds from prior years LDRRM are treated as Trust Liability at the end of the year and duly transferred to a special trust fund account; and
Determine the correct unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and prepare the necessary adjustments.

17.7	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Engineer designate was also advised to continuously update himself with the new guidelines for infrastructure projects and henceforth abide to the provisions of DPWH Dept. Order No. 11 S. 2014

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant take extra care in recording transactions to present fairly the financial statements. Share of the Municipality on local tobacco excise taxes should be recorded as Share from Tobacco Excise Tax and disbursements therefrom should be recorded to their proper expense or asset accounts. Also, the proposed adjusting entry should be prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

We have recommended that the disbursement vouchers and liquidations of the Municipal Treasurer be submitted immediately to the Auditor for verification, otherwise, management must cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445 and Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Henceforth, disbursement vouchers/ liquidation report must be submitted within 10 days after the end of each month together with the journals and reports of disbursement.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor effect a reorganization of the Accounting Office and appoint a regular Municipal Accountant who shall take charge of both the accounting and internal audit services of the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, DPWH Department Order No.22 s.2015 should be utilized in the computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects. Likewise, the members of the Municipal Engineering Office and the Technical Working Group of the Bids and Awards Committee familiarize themselves with the promulgations issued by the DPWH.

We have have recommended that management justify the above noted findings. Henceforth, observe the PS limitation in both budget preparation and budget execution phase pursuant to Section 325 of RA 7160.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the Sangguniang bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC and Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2015, if any. Henceforth, management should observe and abide to the rules and regulations with regards to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund. Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry for the unrecorded taxes withheld to reflect the correct balance of the Due to BIR account.
